reasoning: TYROBP (also called DAP12) is an immunoreceptor‑tyrosine‑based activation motif (ITAM) adaptor that transduces signals from receptors such as TREM2 in myeloid cells and microglia. When TYROBP is functional it promotes downstream activation of SYK, NF‑κB and MAPK pathways, leading to production of pro‑inflammatory cytokines and type‑I interferon responses. Cmpk2 (mitochondrial UMP‑CMP kinase 2) is an interferon‑stimulated gene that is up‑regulated in response to type‑I IFN signaling and other inflammatory cues. Reducing TYROBP levels with CRISPRi diminishes SYK/NF‑κB activation and the consequent interferon‑driven transcriptional program. Consequently, the stimulus that normally drives Cmpk2 expression is weakened, making it likely that Cmpk2 transcription will decrease rather than increase. No direct activating relationship is known, so the expected effect of TYROBP knockdown on Cmpk2 is a reduction in its expression.
